summary: Jiang et al. propose the Transformed Latent Variable MOGP (T-LVMOGP), a scalable framework using Lipschitz-regularised neural networks and stochastic variational inference to model high-dimensional output spaces, outperforming baselines in climate modelling and spatial transcriptomics.

blocks.content: # Transformed Latent Variable Multi-Output Gaussian Processes

source_span.quote: # Transformed Latent Variable Multi-Output Gaussian Processes

blocks.content: Multi-Output Gaussian Processes (MOGPs) provide a principled probabilistic framework for modelling correlated outputs but face scalability bottlenecks when applied to datasets with high-dimensional output spaces.

source_span.quote: Multi-Output Gaussian Processes (MOGPs) provide a principled probabilistic framework for modelling correlated outputs but face scalability bottlenecks when applied to datasets with high-dimensional output spaces.

blocks.content: To maintain tractability, existing methods typically resort to restrictive assumptions, such as employing low-rank or sum-of-separable kernels, which can limit expressiveness.

source_span.quote: To maintain tractability, existing methods typically resort to restrictive assumptions, such as employing low-rank or sum-of-separable kernels, which can limit expressiveness.

blocks.content: We propose the Transformed Latent Variable MOGP (T-LVMOGP), a novel framework that scales MOGPs to a massive number of outputs while preserving the capacity to capture meaningful inter-output dependencies.

source_span.quote: We propose the Transformed Latent Variable MOGP (T-LVMOGP), a novel framework that scales MOGPs to a massive number of outputs while preserving the capacity to capture meaningful inter-output dependencies.

blocks.content: T-LVMOGP constructs a flexible multi-output deep kernel by mapping inputs and output-specific latent variables into an embedding space using a Lipschitz-regularised neural network.

source_span.quote: T-LVMOGP constructs a flexible multi-output deep kernel by mapping inputs and output-specific latent variables into an embedding space using a Lipschitz-regularised neural network.

blocks.content: Combined with stochastic variational inference, our model effectively scales to high-dimensional output settings.

source_span.quote: Combined with stochastic variational inference, our model effectively scales to high-dimensional output settings.

blocks.content: Across diverse benchmarks, including climate modelling with over 10,000 outputs and zero-inflated spatial transcriptomics data, T-LVMOGP outperforms baselines in both predictive accuracy and computational efficiency.

source_span.quote: Across diverse benchmarks, including climate modelling with over 10,000 outputs and zero-inflated spatial transcriptomics data, T-LVMOGP outperforms baselines in both predictive accuracy and computational efficiency.
